content.subject: Add rts_set/rts_unset helpers in sched.rs
content.description: Add unsafe fn rts_set(p: &mut Proc, flag: u32) and rts_unset that capture nr/runnable-state and drop the borrow before calling enqueue/dequeue. Pattern: same as clock::tick NLL drop.

content.subject: Create ipc/message.rs with user-memory copy helpers
content.description: copy_msg_from_user / copy_msg_to_user with user_va_ok bounds check (va non-null, aligned, va+len ≤ 2^48).

content.subject: Create ipc/deadlock.rs
content.description: deadlock_check walking sendto_e/getfrom_e chain with size-2 SEND↔RECV legal-pair via function<<2 trick.

content.subject: Create ipc/notify.rs
content.description: mini_notify with WILLRECEIVE check (immediate delivery via BuildNotifyMessage + MF_DELIVERMSG + rts_unset) or set bit in dst.priv.notify_pending. Never blocks.

content.subject: Create ipc/send.rs
content.description: mini_send (blocking + NonBlocking) — WILLRECEIVE immediate delivery, else NonBlocking ENOTREADY, else deadlock_check + RTS_SENDING + caller_q append.

content.subject: Create ipc/receive.rs
content.description: mini_receive — notify_pending walk, caller_q walk with splice + rts_unset(sender, RTS_SENDING), else block with RTS_RECEIVING + getfrom_e. Sets deliver_msg_vir early.

content.subject: Create ipc/senda.rs (ENOSYS stub)
content.description: Stub returning ENOSYS with TODO(slice 2.6+) comment.

content.subject: Rewrite ipc/mod.rs with full do_ipc dispatcher
content.description: do_ipc decodes frame; resolves caller via current_proc_mut; with_trap_check validates priv/trap_mask; dispatches to mini_*; writes result to frame.x[0]; counter trace every 100. Adds el1_flush_current shim and flush_deliver_msg helper.

content.subject: Wire flush_deliver_msg into EL1→EL0 transitions
content.description: Patch trap.S SVC tail to insert bl el1_flush_current between bl do_ipc and b el1_return_to_user. Add flush calls in sched::reschedule and sched::run.

content.subject: Add install_stub_privs helper for A/B priv slots
content.description: Set up Priv slots 16 (A) and 17 (B) with SRV_T trap_mask and cross-target ipc_to bits. Called from userland_bootstrap before enqueue.

content.subject: Rewrite user_stub.S with sender A and echo B
content.description: Two PC-relative-free stubs: A SENDREC to B (counter in x19); B RECEIVE-ANY then SEND-reply to m_source.

content.subject: Update userland.rs for two code pages + priv wiring
content.description: Two USER_CODE_PAGE_{A,B}, two extern symbol pairs, separate map_4k for each code VA, copy+flush_icache both, call install_stub_privs, set p.priv_id in populate_stub_slot.

content.text: Before writing mod.rs, I need to think through the scheduling flow. Currently `reschedule()` re-enqueues the current proc unconditionally — that's wrong when the caller blocked during IPC (the caller was already dequeued by `rts_set`, and re-enqueueing puts a blocked proc back on the queue). I need to split: `reschedule()` stays for quantum-exhaust, but a new `schedule_next()` picks-and-flushes for the SVC tail.

+/// `SENDREC` is the atomic-send-then-receive primitive used by every
+/// `_syscall()` wrapper. SEND half blocks (or delivers); on success we
+/// mark `MF_REPLY_PEND` and run the RECEIVE half against the same partner.
-    // OK return code, MINIX-style. The SVC restore path puts this in the
+/// Copy a pending IPC message out to the user buffer recorded at
+    // Best-effort; if the user buffer is bad, slice 2.5 has no place to
+    // signal that (the receiver hasn't returned yet). Phase 3 adds
+    // MF_MSGFAILED + signal delivery to handle this. The bounds check
+    // in `copy_msg_to_user` keeps an out-of-range VA from faulting EL1.
+/// SVC-tail shim. `trap.S` calls this between `do_ipc` and
+/// `el1_return_to_user`; it picks the next runnable proc (which may be
+/// the same caller, may be a higher-priority receiver that just
+/// unblocked, or may be someone else entirely if the caller blocked)
+/// and flushes any pending message into the new current proc's user
+    // SAFETY: SVC dispatch context — single-threaded, DAIF.I masked,
+    // no other PROC_TABLE/RUNQ references live at this point.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
 * MINIX 4 aarch64 EL0 → EL1 trap entry + eret stub.
 *
 * Used for:
 *   - EL0 SVC traps (vector slot 8). `vectors.S` branches here unconditionally
 *     for that slot; this stub saves user state into CURRENT_PROC->regs,
 *     decodes ESR_EL1, and dispatches either `do_ipc` (EC = SVC64) or the
 *     unexpected-sync panic helper.
 *   - Returning to EL0. `switch_to_user` and the SVC tail both branch to
 *     `el1_return_to_user`, which restores `tpidr_el1`-pointed regs and erets.
 *
 * Calling convention:
 *   - TPIDR_EL1 always holds `&mut CURRENT_PROC->regs` while we are running
 *     a user context. `switch_to_user` sets it before the first eret; we
 *     never clear it.
 *   - Field offsets below must match `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/context.rs`.
 *     The .equ block is mirrored from the Rust REGS_*_OFFSET constants; the
 *     `const _: () = assert!(size_of::<ArchRegisterFrame> == 272)` canary in
 *     context.rs catches drift in the trailing fields.
 */

    .equ REGS_X_OFFSET,      0
    .equ REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, 248
    .equ REGS_ELR_OFFSET,    256
    .equ REGS_SPSR_OFFSET,   264

    .equ ESR_EC_SVC64,       0x15

    .section .text.trap, "ax", %progbits

/*
 * el0_64_sync_entry — entry stub for vector slot 8 (EL0 AArch64 sync).
 *
 * On entry:
 *   - EL1h, SP = SP_EL1 (kernel stack) is live and usable.
 *   - All EL0 user regs x0..x30 hold caller values, unsaved.
 *   - TPIDR_EL1 = &mut CURRENT_PROC->regs.
 *   - ESR/ELR/SPSR_EL1 describe the trap.
 *
 * We spill x29/x30 to the kernel stack to free two scratch registers
 * (x29 will be the frame pointer; x30 is reloaded from the stack into a
 * scratch reg before being written into the frame). All other user regs
 * are saved directly into the frame via stp/str, then SP_EL0 / ELR_EL1 /
 * SPSR_EL1 follow. EC is decoded from ESR_EL1; SVC64 → do_ipc, anything
 * else → el0_sync_unexpected.
 */
    .global el0_64_sync_entry
    .type el0_64_sync_entry, %function
el0_64_sync_entry:
    // Free x29/x30 for use as scratch (frame pointer + temporary).
    stp     x29, x30, [sp, #-16]!

    // x29 = &frame.
    mrs     x29, tpidr_el1

    // Save x0..x28 into the frame. x29 (frame ptr) cannot be its own base
    // and destination simultaneously, so we save the user's x29/x30 below.
    stp     x0,  x1,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x00]
    stp     x2,  x3,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x10]
    stp     x4,  x5,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x20]
    stp     x6,  x7,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x30]
    stp     x8,  x9,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x40]
    stp     x10, x11, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x50]
    stp     x12, x13, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x60]
    stp     x14, x15, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x70]
    stp     x16, x17, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x80]
    stp     x18, x19, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x90]
    stp     x20, x21, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xA0]
    stp     x22, x23, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xB0]
    stp     x24, x25, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xC0]
    stp     x26, x27, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xD0]
    str     x28,      [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE0]

    // Pop the user's x29 and x30 off the kernel stack into scratch regs
    // and store them into the frame.
    ldp     x16, x17, [sp], #16
    stp     x16, x17, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE8]

    // Save SP_EL0, ELR_EL1, SPSR_EL1.
    mrs     x9,  sp_el0
    mrs     x10, elr_el1
    mrs     x11, spsr_el1
    stp     x9,  x10, [x29, #REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET]
    str     x11,      [x29, #REGS_SPSR_OFFSET]

    // Decode ESR_EL1 EC and dispatch.
    mrs     x12, esr_el1
    lsr     x13, x12, #26
    cmp     x13, #ESR_EC_SVC64
    b.ne    1f

    // SVC64 — call do_ipc(&mut frame).
    mov     x0, x29
    bl      do_ipc
    b       el1_return_to_user

1:  // Non-SVC EL0 sync exception — diagnose and panic.
    // Args: el0_sync_unexpected(esr, elr, far).
    mov     x0, x12              // esr_el1
    mrs     x1, elr_el1
    mrs     x2, far_el1
    bl      el0_sync_unexpected
    // el0_sync_unexpected is -> !; if it ever returns, halt.
2:  wfe
    b       2b
    .size el0_64_sync_entry, . - el0_64_sync_entry

/*
 * el1_return_to_user — restore TPIDR_EL1-pointed frame and eret.
 *
 * Used by:
 *   - proc::sched::switch_to_user (first entry into EL0).
 *   - SVC tail (after do_ipc returns).
 *
 * Both paths leave TPIDR_EL1 pointing at the frame. x9 stays the base
 * throughout; x8 and x9 are restored last (otherwise loading into x9
 * would clobber the base).
 */
    .global el1_return_to_user
    .type el1_return_to_user, %function
el1_return_to_user:
    mrs     x9, tpidr_el1

    // System registers first.
    ldp     x10, x11, [x9, #REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET]   // sp_el0, elr_el1
    ldr     x12,      [x9, #REGS_SPSR_OFFSET]
    msr     sp_el0,   x10
    msr     elr_el1,  x11
    msr     spsr_el1, x12

    // GPRs. Skip x8/x9 here — they'd clobber the base mid-restore.
    ldp     x0,  x1,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x00]
    ldp     x2,  x3,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x10]
    ldp     x4,  x5,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x20]
    ldp     x6,  x7,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x30]
    ldp     x10, x11, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x50]
    ldp     x12, x13, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x60]
    ldp     x14, x15, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x70]
    ldp     x16, x17, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x80]
    ldp     x18, x19, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x90]
    ldp     x20, x21, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xA0]
    ldp     x22, x23, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xB0]
    ldp     x24, x25, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xC0]
    ldp     x26, x27, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xD0]
    ldp     x28, x29, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE0]
    ldr     x30,      [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xF0]

    // Finally x8 and x9 — load x9 last so the base survives until needed.
    ldr     x8,       [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x40]
    ldr     x9,       [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x48]

    eret
    .size el1_return_to_user, . - el1_return_to_user

content.subject: Create ipc/message.rs with user-memory copy helpers
content.description: copy_msg_from_user / copy_msg_to_user with user_va_ok bounds check (va non-null, aligned, va+len ≤ 2^48).

content.subject: Create ipc/deadlock.rs
content.description: deadlock_check walking sendto_e/getfrom_e chain with size-2 SEND↔RECV legal-pair via function<<2 trick.

content.subject: Create ipc/notify.rs
content.description: mini_notify with WILLRECEIVE check (immediate delivery via BuildNotifyMessage + MF_DELIVERMSG + rts_unset) or set bit in dst.priv.notify_pending. Never blocks.

content.subject: Create ipc/send.rs

content.subject: Create ipc/receive.rs
content.description: mini_receive — notify_pending walk, caller_q walk with splice + rts_unset(sender, RTS_SENDING), else block with RTS_RECEIVING + getfrom_e. Sets deliver_msg_vir early.

input.content:
/*
 * EL0 stubs for slice 2.5 ping-pong demo.
 *
 * Two PC-relative-free blobs, each copied into its own physical page by
 * `userland_bootstrap` (mapped EL0 RX at USER_CODE_VA_{A,B}). The stubs
 * exercise all three sync IPC primitives:
 *
 *   Stub A — sender: SENDREC to endpoint B in a loop, incrementing a
 *   counter held in x19 (AAPCS callee-saved; the SVC entry/exit code in
 *   trap.S saves and restores all of x0..x30, so x19 survives the trap).
 *
 *   Stub B — echo server: RECEIVE from ANY, then SEND back to whoever
 *   m_source identifies. Mirrors m_type unchanged.
 *
 * Each stub carves its 104-byte Message buffer out of [SP_EL0 - 104,
 * SP_EL0). SP stays 16-byte aligned (104 rounds to nothing modulo 16;
 * we never sub more than 104 so the stack top remains usable). The stack
 * pages are 4 KiB so we have headroom.
 *
 * Endpoint constants below are the boot endpoints of slots 11 and 12 —
 * `make_endpoint(0, 11) = 11`, `make_endpoint(0, 12) = 12`. ANY's encoding
 * is `(1 << 14) - 1 = 0x3FFF` (ENDPOINT_SLOT_TOP at generation 0).
 */

    .equ SEND,        1
    .equ RECEIVE,     2
    .equ SENDREC,     3
    .equ ANY,         0x3FFF
    .equ ENDPOINT_A,  11
    .equ ENDPOINT_B,  12
    .equ MSG_SIZE,    104                  // sizeof(Message); fits in `mov` imm12

/* ---- Stub A: sender ----------------------------------------------------- */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_a, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_a_start
    .type _user_stub_a_start, %object
_user_stub_a_start:
    mov     x19, #0                         // persistent message counter
1:
    // Build Message at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0): m_source=0, m_type=counter.
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    str     wzr, [x9, #0]                   // m_source — kernel overwrites
    str     w19, [x9, #4]                   // m_type   — counter
    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_B
    mov     x1, #SENDREC
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    add     x19, x19, #1
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_a_end
_user_stub_a_end:
    .size _user_stub_a_start, _user_stub_a_end - _user_stub_a_start

/* ---- Stub B: echo server ----------------------------------------------- */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_b, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_b_start
    .type _user_stub_b_start, %object
_user_stub_b_start:
1:
    // Build Message at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0). Kernel fills it on RECEIVE.
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    mov     x0, #ANY
    mov     x1, #RECEIVE
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    // Reply: read m_source out of the delivered message, SEND back with
    // the same m_type already in [x9 + 4].
    ldr     w10, [x9, #0]                   // m_source (signed Endpoint)
    sxtw    x10, w10
    mov     x0, x10
    mov     x1, #SEND
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_b_end
_user_stub_b_end:
    .size _user_stub_b_start, _user_stub_b_end - _user_stub_b_start

 * Each stub carves its 104-byte Message buffer out of [SP_EL0 - 104,
 * SP_EL0). SP stays 16-byte aligned (104 rounds to nothing modulo 16;
 * we never sub more than 104 so the stack top remains usable). The stack
 * pages are 4 KiB so we have headroom.
 *
 * Endpoint constants below are the boot endpoints of slots 11 and 12 —
 * `make_endpoint(0, 11) = 11`, `make_endpoint(0, 12) = 12`. ANY's encoding
 * is `(1 << 14) - 1 = 0x3FFF` (ENDPOINT_SLOT_TOP at generation 0).
 */

    .equ SEND,        1
    .equ RECEIVE,     2
    .equ SENDREC,     3
    .equ ANY,         0x3FFF
    .equ ENDPOINT_A,  11
    .equ ENDPOINT_B,  12
    .equ MSG_SIZE,    104                  // sizeof(Message); fits in `mov` imm12

/* ---- Stub A: sender ----------------------------------------------------- */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_a, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_a_start
    .type _user_stub_a_start, %object
_user_stub_a_start:
    mov     x19, #0                         // persistent message counter
1:
    // Build Message at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0): m_source=0, m_type=counter.
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    str     wzr, [x9, #0]                   // m_source — kernel overwrites
    str     w19, [x9, #4]                   // m_type   — counter
    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_B
    mov     x1, #SENDREC
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    add     x19, x19, #1
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_a_end
_user_stub_a_end:
    .size _user_stub_a_start, _user_stub_a_end - _user_stub_a_start

/* ---- Stub B: echo server ----------------------------------------------- */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_b, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_b_start
    .type _user_stub_b_start, %object
_user_stub_b_start:
1:
    // Build Message at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0). Kernel fills it on RECEIVE.
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    mov     x0, #ANY
    mov     x1, #RECEIVE
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    // Reply: read m_source out of the delivered message, SEND back with
    // the same m_type already in [x9 + 4].
    ldr     w10, [x9, #0]                   // m_source (signed Endpoint)
    sxtw    x10, w10
    mov     x0, x10
    mov     x1, #SEND
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_b_end
_user_stub_b_end:
    .size _user_stub_b_start, _user_stub_b_end - _user_stub_b_start

/*
 * EL0 stub task for slice 2.3.
 *
 * Lives in `.rodata.user_stub` inside the kernel image; `userland_bootstrap`
 * copies these bytes into the user code page (mapped EL0 RX at
 * USER_CODE_VA) before erets. Every instruction here is PC-relative-free, so
 * the same encoding works at any VA — no relocations required.
 *
 * What it does:
 *   - x0 = 0x7FFE  (boot endpoint for SYSTEM, i.e. make_endpoint(0, -2))
 *   - x1 = 3        (SENDREC IPC primitive)
 *   - x2 = 0        (no message pointer; the slice-2.3 do_ipc stub ignores it)
 *   - svc #0        (trap into the kernel SVC handler)
 *   - b _user_stub_start  (loop forever to keep generating do_ipc calls)
 */

    .section .rodata.user_stub, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_start
    .type _user_stub_start, %object
_user_stub_start:
    mov     x0, #0x7FFE
    mov     x1, #3
    mov     x2, xzr
    svc     #0
    b       _user_stub_start
    .global _user_stub_end
_user_stub_end:
    .size _user_stub_start, _user_stub_end - _user_stub_start

content.text: Let me verify `dump_tables` won't change behavior if called after `userland_bootstrap`, then move the call so it shows the slice-2.5 stub privs.

content.subject: Add rts_set/rts_unset helpers in sched.rs
content.description: Add unsafe fn rts_set(p: &mut Proc, flag: u32) and rts_unset that capture nr/runnable-state and drop the borrow before calling enqueue/dequeue. Pattern: same as clock::tick NLL drop.

content.subject: Create ipc/message.rs with user-memory copy helpers
content.description: copy_msg_from_user / copy_msg_to_user with user_va_ok bounds check (va non-null, aligned, va+len ≤ 2^48).

content.subject: Create ipc/deadlock.rs
content.description: deadlock_check walking sendto_e/getfrom_e chain with size-2 SEND↔RECV legal-pair via function<<2 trick.

content.subject: Create ipc/notify.rs
content.description: mini_notify with WILLRECEIVE check (immediate delivery via BuildNotifyMessage + MF_DELIVERMSG + rts_unset) or set bit in dst.priv.notify_pending. Never blocks.

content.subject: Create ipc/send.rs
content.description: mini_send (blocking + NonBlocking) — WILLRECEIVE immediate delivery, else NonBlocking ENOTREADY, else deadlock_check + RTS_SENDING + caller_q append.

content.subject: Create ipc/receive.rs
content.description: mini_receive — notify_pending walk, caller_q walk with splice + rts_unset(sender, RTS_SENDING), else block with RTS_RECEIVING + getfrom_e. Sets deliver_msg_vir early.

content.subject: Create ipc/senda.rs (ENOSYS stub)
content.description: Stub returning ENOSYS with TODO(slice 2.6+) comment.

content.subject: Rewrite ipc/mod.rs with full do_ipc dispatcher
content.description: do_ipc decodes frame; resolves caller via current_proc_mut; with_trap_check validates priv/trap_mask; dispatches to mini_*; writes result to frame.x[0]; counter trace every 100. Adds el1_flush_current shim and flush_deliver_msg helper.

content.subject: Wire flush_deliver_msg into EL1→EL0 transitions
content.description: Patch trap.S SVC tail to insert bl el1_flush_current between bl do_ipc and b el1_return_to_user. Add flush calls in sched::reschedule and sched::run.

content.subject: Add install_stub_privs helper for A/B priv slots
content.description: Set up Priv slots 16 (A) and 17 (B) with SRV_T trap_mask and cross-target ipc_to bits. Called from userland_bootstrap before enqueue.

content.subject: Rewrite user_stub.S with sender A and echo B
content.description: Two PC-relative-free stubs: A SENDREC to B (counter in x19); B RECEIVE-ANY then SEND-reply to m_source.

content.subject: Update userland.rs for two code pages + priv wiring
content.description: Two USER_CODE_PAGE_{A,B}, two extern symbol pairs, separate map_4k for each code VA, copy+flush_icache both, call install_stub_privs, set p.priv_id in populate_stub_slot.

content.subject: Run host tests + QEMU smoke test
content.description: cargo test --workspace; cargo kernel-aarch64; timeout 8 cargo run boots and grep verifies [ipc N] lines for call=1,2,3 all result=0.

content.subject: Update docs/plan.md + CLAUDE.md
content.description: Flip slice 2.5 marker to shipped, slice 2.6 to ◀ next. Add 3 Code Conventions bullets to CLAUDE.md.

content.content: Updated task #13 status
Updated task #14 status

440	  the new `kernel/src/ipc/` module prints each call and stores `OK` in
441	  `frame.x[0]`. `proc::sched::switch_to_user` wraps the eret. Verified
442	  in QEMU: `do_ipc[N]: call_nr=3 src_dst=32766 msg=0x0...` repeats
443	  ~16K times per second.
444	- **Slice 2.4** ◀ ready (branch `feature/phase-2-4-gicv3-timer-runqueues`,
445	  pending merge) — GICv3 + ARM generic timer + run queues. New
446	  `arch/aarch64/{gic,timer,irq}.rs` + `interrupt.S` bring up GICD/GICR
447	  (QEMU virt cortex-a72, `-machine virt,gic-version=3`), enable PPI 27
448	  (EL1 virtual timer) at 100 Hz, and route the IRQ through vector slot
449	  9 → `el0_64_irq_entry` (mirrors slice 2.3's slot-8 treatment) →
450	  `do_irq` → ICC_IAR1_EL1 ack / `clock::tick` / ICC_EOIR1_EL1. New
451	  `proc::sched` adds priority-banded FIFO run queues (`enqueue`,
452	  `dequeue`, `pick_proc`, `reschedule`, `run`) chained through
453	  `Proc::next_ready: Option<ProcNr>`. New `kernel/src/clock.rs` owns
454	  the per-tick handler — prints `current_proc.name[0]` to PL011, then
455	  decrements `quantum_left` and triggers `reschedule` on
456	  `RTS_NO_QUANTUM`. `userland_bootstrap` now stages two EL0 stubs (A
457	  and B at `0x40_0000`/`0x41_0000` sharing one code page, distinct
458	  stacks at `0x80_0000`/`0x81_0000`); SPSR drops to `0x340` so IRQs
459	  are unmasked at EL0. Verified in QEMU: 171 A ticks vs 170 B ticks
460	  over ~3.4 s, clean `AAAAA BBBBB AAAAA …` 5-per-quantum bursts with
461	  `do_ipc[N]` SVC traces interleaved (proves SVC + IRQ paths coexist).
462	- **Slice 2.5** ◀ next — IPC primitives (`mini_send`, `mini_receive`,
463	  `mini_notify`, `mini_senda`, `mini_sendnb`, `deadlock_check`). Two
464	  tasks ping-pong messages. **This is the Phase 2 milestone.**
465	- **Slice 2.6** — Kernel-call dispatch and the minimum `SYS_*` set
466	  (`SYS_GETINFO` + 13 stubs returning `OK`/`ENOSYS`). A stub task
467	  `SENDREC`s `SYSTEM` for `SYS_GETINFO` and gets a populated reply.
468	
469	Aggregate scope:

file.content:
the new `kernel/src/ipc/` module prints each call and stores `OK` in
  `frame.x[0]`. `proc::sched::switch_to_user` wraps the eret. Verified
  in QEMU: `do_ipc[N]: call_nr=3 src_dst=32766 msg=0x0...` repeats
  ~16K times per second.
- **Slice 2.4** ◀ ready (branch `feature/phase-2-4-gicv3-timer-runqueues`,
  pending merge) — GICv3 + ARM generic timer + run queues. New
  `arch/aarch64/{gic,timer,irq}.rs` + `interrupt.S` bring up GICD/GICR
  (QEMU virt cortex-a72, `-machine virt,gic-version=3`), enable PPI 27
  (EL1 virtual timer) at 100 Hz, and route the IRQ through vector slot
  9 → `el0_64_irq_entry` (mirrors slice 2.3's slot-8 treatment) →
  `do_irq` → ICC_IAR1_EL1 ack / `clock::tick` / ICC_EOIR1_EL1. New
  `proc::sched` adds priority-banded FIFO run queues (`enqueue`,
  `dequeue`, `pick_proc`, `reschedule`, `run`) chained through
  `Proc::next_ready: Option<ProcNr>`. New `kernel/src/clock.rs` owns
  the per-tick handler — prints `current_proc.name[0]` to PL011, then
  decrements `quantum_left` and triggers `reschedule` on
  `RTS_NO_QUANTUM`. `userland_bootstrap` now stages two EL0 stubs (A
  and B at `0x40_0000`/`0x41_0000` sharing one code page, distinct
  stacks at `0x80_0000`/`0x81_0000`); SPSR drops to `0x340` so IRQs
  are unmasked at EL0. Verified in QEMU: 171 A ticks vs 170 B ticks
  over ~3.4 s, clean `AAAAA BBBBB AAAAA …` 5-per-quantum bursts with
  `do_ipc[N]` SVC traces interleaved (proves SVC + IRQ paths coexist).
- **Slice 2.5** ◀ next — IPC primitives (`mini_send`, `mini_receive`,
  `mini_notify`, `mini_senda`, `mini_sendnb`, `deadlock_check`). Two
  tasks ping-pong messages. **This is the Phase 2 milestone.**
- **Slice 2.6** — Kernel-call dispatch and the minimum `SYS_*` set
  (`SYS_GETINFO` + 13 stubs returning `OK`/`ENOSYS`). A stub task
  `SENDREC`s `SYSTEM` for `SYS_GETINFO` and gets a populated reply.

Aggregate scope:

- **Slice 2.5** ◀ next — IPC primitives (`mini_send`, `mini_receive`,
  `mini_notify`, `mini_senda`, `mini_sendnb`, `deadlock_check`). Two
  tasks ping-pong messages. **This is the Phase 2 milestone.**
- **Slice 2.6** — Kernel-call dispatch and the minimum `SYS_*` set
  (`SYS_GETINFO` + 13 stubs returning `OK`/`ENOSYS`). A stub task
  `SENDREC`s `SYSTEM` for `SYS_GETINFO` and gets a populated reply.
input.new_string:
- **Slice 2.5** ◀ ready (branch `feature/phase-2-5-ipc-primitives`,
  pending merge) — IPC primitives in Rust. New `kernel/src/ipc/{mod,
  message, send, receive, notify, senda, deadlock}.rs`: `do_ipc`
  dispatcher with trap-mask gating, `mini_send` / `mini_receive` /
  `mini_notify` / `mini_sendnb` faithful to MINIX 3 `kernel/proc.c`,
  `deadlock_check` (size-2 SEND↔RECV legalization via the `function<<2`
  trick), `mini_senda` stub returning `ENOSYS` (deferred to a later
  slice; no Phase-2 consumer). New `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset`
  capture `nr` and end the borrow before calling `enqueue`/`dequeue`
  so RTS transitions stay run-queue-coherent. New `sched::schedule_next`
  picks the highest-priority runnable proc, parks its frame in
  `TPIDR_EL1`, and flushes any pending `MF_DELIVERMSG` into the user
  buffer at `Proc::deliver_msg_vir` — invoked from the SVC tail via the
  new `el1_svc_tail` shim (`trap.S` now reads `bl do_ipc; bl
  el1_svc_tail; b el1_return_to_user`) and from `reschedule` + `run`.
  `Proc` gains `deliver_msg_vir: u64`. User-memory IPC reads via
  `core::ptr::read_volatile` with a coarse `USER_VA_TOP = 1 << 48`
  bounds check; fault recovery is Phase 3. `user_stub.S` rewritten:
  two PC-relative-free blobs in separate sections — stub A
  (`SENDREC` to endpoint B, persistent counter in `x19`), stub B
  (`RECEIVE` from `ANY` then `SEND`-reply to `m_source`). `userland.rs`
  installs two physical code pages, four `map_4k` calls, plus
  `install_stub_privs` filling priv slots 16/17 with
  `trap_mask = SRV_T` and `ipc_to` cross-targeting A↔B. Verified in
  QEMU over 8 s: ~897K IPC ops; ~2990 each of `call=1`/`2`/`3` with
  every line `result=0`; no panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`; `A`/`B`
  clock-tick interleaving from slice 2.4 still visible. **Phase 2
  milestone reached.**
- **Slice 2.6** ◀ next — Kernel-call dispatch and the minimum `SYS_*`
  set (`SYS_GETINFO` + 13 stubs returning `OK`/`ENOSYS`). A stub task
  `SENDREC`s `SYSTEM` for `SYS_GETINFO` and gets a populated reply.

68	## Code Conventions
69	
70	- Kernel `unsafe` blocks require `// SAFETY:` comments documenting the invariant
71	- IPC linked lists use `Option<ProcNr>` indices into static arrays, not raw pointers
72	- Message types are defined in `kernel-shared` and shared across all crates
73	- Assembly is confined to `.S` files (assembled via `cc` crate in `build.rs`); use `core::arch::asm!` only for single-instruction operations
74	- New `.S` files must be added to `kernel/build.rs`'s `sources` array; offset blocks (`.equ REGS_*_OFFSET …`) are duplicated per-file since there is no cross-`.S` include
75	- To end a `&mut` borrow before an `unsafe` call that re-borrows the same static, capture state into locals (bool / scalar) and rely on NLL — `drop(&mut x)` is a no-op and triggers a `dropping_references` warning
76	- Run-queue admission is decoupled from boot: `IMAGE.runnable` marks IPC reachability; only `proc::sched::enqueue` puts a proc in the scheduler's run queue
77	- Static mutable tables use `UnsafeCell<[T; N]>` inside a `#[repr(transparent)]` newtype with `unsafe impl Sync`; document the single-threaded-boot invariant in the `// SAFETY:` comment
78	- Custom `Display` impls that must honor `{:<width$}` render through a stack buffer (`arrayvec::ArrayString<N>`) and call `f.pad(s)` — `write!(f, ...)` from inside `Display::fmt` ignores the outer width spec
79	- Forward declarations intended for later slices (constants, fields, re-exports) get module-level `#![allow(dead_code)]` with a one-line comment naming the consuming slice
80

file.content:
## Code Conventions

- Kernel `unsafe` blocks require `// SAFETY:` comments documenting the invariant
- IPC linked lists use `Option<ProcNr>` indices into static arrays, not raw pointers
- Message types are defined in `kernel-shared` and shared across all crates
- Assembly is confined to `.S` files (assembled via `cc` crate in `build.rs`); use `core::arch::asm!` only for single-instruction operations
- New `.S` files must be added to `kernel/build.rs`'s `sources` array; offset blocks (`.equ REGS_*_OFFSET …`) are duplicated per-file since there is no cross-`.S` include
- To end a `&mut` borrow before an `unsafe` call that re-borrows the same static, capture state into locals (bool / scalar) and rely on NLL — `drop(&mut x)` is a no-op and triggers a `dropping_references` warning
- Run-queue admission is decoupled from boot: `IMAGE.runnable` marks IPC reachability; only `proc::sched::enqueue` puts a proc in the scheduler's run queue
- Static mutable tables use `UnsafeCell<[T; N]>` inside a `#[repr(transparent)]` newtype with `unsafe impl Sync`; document the single-threaded-boot invariant in the `// SAFETY:` comment
- Custom `Display` impls that must honor `{:<width$}` render through a stack buffer (`arrayvec::ArrayString<N>`) and call `f.pad(s)` — `write!(f, ...)` from inside `Display::fmt` ignores the outer width spec
- Forward declarations intended for later slices (constants, fields, re-exports) get module-level `#![allow(dead_code)]` with a one-line comment naming the consuming slice

## Documentation

`docs/plan.md` tracks slice status with three markers: `◀ next` (unstarted), `◀ ready (branch ..., pending merge)` (implemented but unmerged), `✓ shipped (PR #N, merged YYYY-MM-DD)` (merged). Flip the previous slice forward and slide `◀ next` ahead as part of each slice's PR.

## Documentation
input.new_string:
- Forward declarations intended for later slices (constants, fields, re-exports) get module-level `#![allow(dead_code)]` with a one-line comment naming the consuming slice
- IPC primitives take an explicit `&mut [Proc; N_PROC_SLOTS]` (and `&mut [Priv; NR_SYS_PROCS]`) slice; only `ipc::do_ipc` materializes those from `PROC_TABLE` / `PRIV_TABLE` via `proc_table_mut_slice` / `priv_table_mut_slice`. Keeps each primitive testable in isolation and dodges the two-`&mut`-from-one-`UnsafeCell` UB hazard
- Every EL1 → EL0 transition (SVC tail via `el1_svc_tail`, `sched::reschedule`, `sched::run`) calls `sched::schedule_next`, which flushes `Proc::deliver_msg` to the user buffer at `Proc::deliver_msg_vir` and clears `MF_DELIVERMSG` before resuming
- IPC blocking pairs with the new `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset` helpers — they capture `nr`, end the `&mut Proc` borrow, then call `enqueue` / `dequeue` so RTS state and the run queue stay in sync. Same NLL-capture pattern slice 2.4 used in `clock::tick`
- `kernel/build.rs` skips assembly when `CARGO_CFG_TARGET_OS != "none"` so `cargo check --workspace` / `cargo test --workspace` keep working on host. The kernel's real modules are gated by `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` in `main.rs` regardless

## Documentation

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# MINIX 4

MINIX 4 is a learning OS with a Rust microkernel, based on MINIX 3's architecture.

## Project Overview

- **Kernel:** Rust (no_std, no_main), greenfield microkernel
- **Architecture:** aarch64 primary (Apple Silicon / QEMU virt), x86_64 secondary
- **Servers:** PM, VFS, VM, RS, DS, SCHED as user-space Rust processes
- **Drivers:** VirtIO (MMIO for aarch64, PCI for x86_64) as user-space processes
- **C library:** musl-libc fork with MINIX IPC syscall wrappers
- **License:** BSD-2-Clause only (no GPL code)
- **Platform:** QEMU as primary target

## Reference Codebase

MINIX 4's architecture is based on MINIX 3. When the docs reference "MINIX 3 source",
they mean paths within the MINIX 3 source tree (e.g., `kernel/proc.c` means the
`kernel/proc.c` file in a MINIX 3 checkout). Key reference files:

- `kernel/proc.c` -- IPC implementation (mini_send, mini_receive, deadlock detection)
- `kernel/proc.h`, `priv.h` -- Process and privilege structures
- `kernel/system.c` -- Kernel call dispatch
- `include/minix/ipc.h` -- Message structure definitions
- `include/minix/com.h`, `callnr.h` -- Server endpoints, call numbers
- `lib/libc/sys/*.c` -- POSIX syscall wrappers (template for musl adaptation)
- `lib/libsys/sef.c` -- SEF framework (template for server-rt)

The MINIX 3 source is available at https://github.com/Stichting-MINIX-Research-Foundation/minix

## Build

```sh
# Build kernel for aarch64 (primary target)
cargo kernel-aarch64

# Boot in QEMU (cargo runner wires tools/qemu-run.sh). The kernel runs
# indefinitely once EL0 starts (slice 2.4+), so `timeout` is mandatory.
# Redirect to a file when you need to grep tick output -- live tail loses lines.
timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release

# Build kernel for x86_64
cargo kernel-x86_64
```

## Architecture

See `docs/architecture.md` for the full system design. Key concepts:

- **Microkernel:** Only IPC, scheduling, interrupt dispatch, and memory protection in kernel
- **Message passing:** 6 IPC primitives (SEND, RECEIVE, SENDREC, NOTIFY, SENDNB, SENDA)
- **User-space servers:** All OS services (file system, process management, memory management) run as separate processes communicating via IPC
- **Privilege model:** Fine-grained bitmaps control which processes can communicate and what kernel calls they can make

## Workspace Crates

| Crate | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `kernel` | Microkernel (no_std) |
| `kernel-shared` | Message types, endpoints, call numbers shared between kernel and userspace |
| `minix-ipc` | User-space IPC library (SVC/SYSCALL asm stubs) |
| `server-rt` | Server runtime / SEF framework |
| `servers/*` | System servers (PM, VFS, VM, RS, DS, SCHED) |
| `drivers/*` | Device drivers (VirtIO block/net/console, memory) |
| `fs/*` | File system servers (MFS, PFS) |
| `userland/*` | User programs (init, sh, coreutils) |

## Code Conventions

- Kernel `unsafe` blocks require `// SAFETY:` comments documenting the invariant
- IPC linked lists use `Option<ProcNr>` indices into static arrays, not raw pointers
- Message types are defined in `kernel-shared` and shared across all crates
- Assembly is confined to `.S` files (assembled via `cc` crate in `build.rs`); use `core::arch::asm!` only for single-instruction operations
- New `.S` files must be added to `kernel/build.rs`'s `sources` array; offset blocks (`.equ REGS_*_OFFSET …`) are duplicated per-file since there is no cross-`.S` include
- To end a `&mut` borrow before an `unsafe` call that re-borrows the same static, capture state into locals (bool / scalar) and rely on NLL — `drop(&mut x)` is a no-op and triggers a `dropping_references` warning
- Run-queue admission is decoupled from boot: `IMAGE.runnable` marks IPC reachability; only `proc::sched::enqueue` puts a proc in the scheduler's run queue
- Static mutable tables use `UnsafeCell<[T; N]>` inside a `#[repr(transparent)]` newtype with `unsafe impl Sync`; document the single-threaded-boot invariant in the `// SAFETY:` comment
- Custom `Display` impls that must honor `{:<width$}` render through a stack buffer (`arrayvec::ArrayString<N>`) and call `f.pad(s)` — `write!(f, ...)` from inside `Display::fmt` ignores the outer width spec
- Forward declarations intended for later slices (constants, fields, re-exports) get module-level `#![allow(dead_code)]` with a one-line comment naming the consuming slice

## Documentation

`docs/plan.md` tracks slice status with three markers: `◀ next` (unstarted), `◀ ready (branch ..., pending merge)` (implemented but unmerged), `✓ shipped (PR #N, merged YYYY-MM-DD)` (merged). Flip the previous slice forward and slide `◀ next` ahead as part of each slice's PR.

lines: - Static mutable tables use `UnsafeCell<[T; N]>` inside a `#[repr(transparent)]` newtype with `unsafe impl Sync`; document the single-threaded-boot invariant in the `// SAFETY:` comment

- Custom `Display` impls that must honor `{:<width$}` render through a stack buffer (`arrayvec::ArrayString<N>`) and call `f.pad(s)` — `write!(f, ...)` from inside `Display::fmt` ignores the outer width spec
- Forward declarations intended for later slices (constants, fields, re-exports) get module-level `#![allow(dead_code)]` with a one-line comment naming the consuming slice

+- IPC primitives take an explicit `&mut [Proc; N_PROC_SLOTS]` (and `&mut [Priv; NR_SYS_PROCS]`) slice; only `ipc::do_ipc` materializes those from `PROC_TABLE` / `PRIV_TABLE` via `proc_table_mut_slice` / `priv_table_mut_slice`. Keeps each primitive testable in isolation and dodges the two-`&mut`-from-one-`UnsafeCell` UB hazard

+- Every EL1 → EL0 transition (SVC tail via `el1_svc_tail`, `sched::reschedule`, `sched::run`) calls `sched::schedule_next`, which flushes `Proc::deliver_msg` to the user buffer at `Proc::deliver_msg_vir` and clears `MF_DELIVERMSG` before resuming

+- IPC blocking pairs with the new `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset` helpers — they capture `nr`, end the `&mut Proc` borrow, then call `enqueue` / `dequeue` so RTS state and the run queue stay in sync. Same NLL-capture pattern slice 2.4 used in `clock::tick`

diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
index 71e86dd..4e18900 100644
--- a/CLAUDE.md
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -77,6 +77,10 @@ See `docs/architecture.md` for the full system design. Key concepts:
 - Static mutable tables use `UnsafeCell<[T; N]>` inside a `#[repr(transparent)]` newtype with `unsafe impl Sync`; document the single-threaded-boot invariant in the `// SAFETY:` comment
 - Custom `Display` impls that must honor `{:<width$}` render through a stack buffer (`arrayvec::ArrayString<N>`) and call `f.pad(s)` — `write!(f, ...)` from inside `Display::fmt` ignores the outer width spec
 - Forward declarations intended for later slices (constants, fields, re-exports) get module-level `#![allow(dead_code)]` with a one-line comment naming the consuming slice
+- IPC primitives take an explicit `&mut [Proc; N_PROC_SLOTS]` (and `&mut [Priv; NR_SYS_PROCS]`) slice; only `ipc::do_ipc` materializes those from `PROC_TABLE` / `PRIV_TABLE` via `proc_table_mut_slice` / `priv_table_mut_slice`. Keeps each primitive testable in isolation and dodges the two-`&mut`-from-one-`UnsafeCell` UB hazard
+- Every EL1 → EL0 transition (SVC tail via `el1_svc_tail`, `sched::reschedule`, `sched::run`) calls `sched::schedule_next`, which flushes `Proc::deliver_msg` to the user buffer at `Proc::deliver_msg_vir` and clears `MF_DELIVERMSG` before resuming
+- IPC blocking pairs with the new `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset` helpers — they capture `nr`, end the `&mut Proc` borrow, then call `enqueue` / `dequeue` so RTS state and the run queue stay in sync. Same NLL-capture pattern slice 2.4 used in `clock::tick`
+- `kernel/build.rs` skips assembly when `CARGO_CFG_TARGET_OS != "none"` so `cargo check --workspace` / `cargo test --workspace` keep working on host. The kernel's real modules are gated by `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` in `main.rs` regardless
 
 ## Documentation

lastPrompt: work on slice 2.5 of phase 2 in @docs/plan.md

content.text:
Slice 2.5 is complete and verified. Summary:

**What landed**
- 7 new files in `kernel/src/ipc/`: `mod.rs` (dispatcher + `el1_svc_tail` shim + counter trace + `flush_deliver_msg`), `message.rs` (user-VA copy + bounds check), `send.rs` (`mini_send` blocking/NB + `caller_q` append), `receive.rs` (notify-pending walk + `caller_q` splice + SENDREC mid-flight handling), `notify.rs` (immediate `WILLRECEIVE` delivery or deferred `notify_pending` bit), `senda.rs` (`ENOSYS` stub per design), `deadlock.rs` (size-2 SEND↔RECV legalization via the `function<<2` trick).
- `Proc::deliver_msg_vir: u64` field added.
- `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset` capture `nr` and end the borrow before `enqueue`/`dequeue`; `sched::schedule_next` picks-and-flushes; `reschedule` now skips re-enqueue when cur is also blocked.
- `trap.S` SVC tail: `bl do_ipc; bl el1_svc_tail; b el1_return_to_user`.
- `user_stub.S` rewritten as two PC-relative-free blobs (sender A in `x19` counter, echo B). `userland.rs` allocates two code pages + two stack pages, and `install_stub_privs` fills priv slots 16/17 (`trap_mask=SRV_T`, `ipc_to` cross-targeting A↔B).
- `kernel/build.rs` skips assembly on host targets so `cargo check --workspace` and `cargo test --workspace` keep working.
- `docs/plan.md` flipped (2.4 → shipped, 2.5 → ready, 2.6 → next) and `CLAUDE.md` got 4 new convention bullets.

**Verification** — `timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release`:
- 8973 `[ipc N]` trace lines; ~2990 each of `call=1` (B→A SEND), `call=2` (B RECEIVE-ANY), `call=3` (A→B SENDREC).
- Every complete line returns `result=0`; no panic, no `el0_sync_unexpected`.
- Slice-2.4 A/B clock-tick interleaving still visible.
- `cargo test --workspace`: 25 kernel-shared tests pass; no regressions.

**Files changed**: 11 modified, 6 new, +624/-143 lines.

Per the auto-mode rule in your global CLAUDE.md, I've stopped here without committing. The branch is `feature/phase-2-5-ipc-primitives`. Want me to commit + push + open the PR, or any tweaks first?

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
phase 2.5: IPC primitives + ping-pong demo

Implements the Phase 2 milestone: faithful Rust translations of MINIX 3's
synchronous IPC primitives (mini_send, mini_receive, mini_notify,
mini_sendnb, deadlock_check; mini_senda stubbed to ENOSYS) plus the
SVC-tail + scheduler plumbing needed for two EL0 tasks to exchange
messages.

New kernel/src/ipc/{mod,message,send,receive,notify,senda,deadlock}.rs.
do_ipc gates on Priv::trap_mask, dispatches to the per-primitive handlers
(all take an explicit &mut [Proc] / &mut [Priv] slice for isolation +
future testability), and writes the result to frame.x[0]. SENDREC is a
SEND half + RECEIVE half against the same partner with MF_REPLY_PEND
bookkeeping in between. deadlock_check is the MINIX 3 algorithm verbatim
with the function<<2 trick to legalize the size-2 SEND<->RECV rendezvous.

sched gains rts_set/rts_unset (capture nr, end the &mut Proc borrow,
then call enqueue/dequeue so RTS transitions stay run-queue-coherent)
and schedule_next (picks the highest-priority runnable proc, parks its
frame in TPIDR_EL1, flushes any pending MF_DELIVERMSG into the user
buffer). reschedule no longer re-enqueues a now-blocked cur. Every
EL1->EL0 transition goes through schedule_next: SVC tail via a new
el1_svc_tail Rust shim wired into trap.S (bl do_ipc; bl el1_svc_tail;
b el1_return_to_user), plus reschedule and run.

Proc gains deliver_msg_vir: u64 (user VA captured at RECEIVE-time,
consumed by flush_deliver_msg). User-memory copies go through
core::ptr::read_volatile/write_volatile with a coarse USER_VA_TOP =
1 << 48 bounds check; phase 3 adds real fault recovery.

user_stub.S rewritten as two PC-relative-free blobs in their own
sections: stub A (sender, SENDREC to endpoint B with a counter in x19
that survives the SVC via AAPCS callee-save), stub B (echo, RECEIVE
from ANY then SEND-reply to m_source). userland.rs allocates a
distinct physical page per stub, adds the matching map_4k calls, and
installs priv slots 16/17 (trap_mask=SRV_T, ipc_to cross-targeting
A<->B and nothing else).

kernel/build.rs skips the .S assembly step when CARGO_CFG_TARGET_OS !=
"none" so `cargo check --workspace` / `cargo test --workspace` keep
working on the host. Kernel modules themselves remain cfg-gated in
main.rs.

Verified in QEMU (timeout 8 cargo run): ~897K IPC ops; ~2990 each of
call=1/2/3 with every line result=0; no panic/el0_sync_unexpected;
slice 2.4 A/B clock-tick interleaving still visible. cargo test
--workspace: 25/25 pass, no regressions.

docs/plan.md flipped (2.4 shipped, 2.5 ready, 2.6 next). CLAUDE.md adds
four Code Conventions bullets capturing the patterns introduced here.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
